question: [File: 'File Name: src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: SetterRatifier.isRatified / HashLib.hashMarket] Can an unprivileged market creator under the precondition that a market is created with MAX_COLLATERALS (128) collateralParams and the SetterRatifier is used as the offer ratifier trigger take() → IRatifier.isRatified() → HashLib.hashOffer() → HashLib.hashMarket() which iterates over all 128 CollateralParams entries in a for-loop, violating the invariant that every take() call must complete within a bounded gas budget regardless of market configuration, causing scoped impact: gas griefing DoS where any taker attempting to fill an offer in a 128-collateral market pays unbounded gas proportional to collateralParams.length? Proof idea: fuzz collateralParams.length from 1 to 128 and measure gas consumed by take() with SetterRatifier, assert gas grows linearly and can exceed block gas limit at max collaterals.

[File: 'File Name: src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: HashLib.isLeaf] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that a valid SetterRatifier root is ratified and a Merkle proof of maximum depth (height=20, proof.length=20) is supplied trigger isRatified() → HashLib.isLeaf() which iterates proof.length times in a for-loop, violating the invariant that ratifier validation gas cost must be bounded and predictable, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where a taker composing a multicall with many take() calls each using depth-20 proofs exhausts block gas? Proof idea: fuzz proof.length from 0 to 20 and measure gas consumed by isRatified(), assert gas scales linearly with proof depth and document the maximum gas cost at height=20.

[File: 'File Name: src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: HashLib.hashMarket / HashLib.hashOffer] Can an unprivileged maker under the precondition that a market with 128 collateralParams is created and the maker ratifies a root via setIsRootRatified() trigger isRatified() → hashOffer() → hashMarket() → loop over 128 CollateralParams hashes, violating the invariant that the gas cost of offer validation must not be controllable by the market creator to grief takers, causing scoped impact: a market creator can deploy a 128-collateral market specifically to make every take() call in that market prohibitively expensive for takers? Proof idea: create market with 128 collaterals, measure gas of take() with SetterRatifier vs EcrecoverRatifier, assert SetterRatifier gas overhead is O(n) in collateralParams.length.

[File: 'File Name: src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.touchMarket] Can an unprivileged market creator under the precondition that they supply a Market struct with collateralParams.length == MAX_COLLATERALS (128) trigger touchMarket() which iterates the full collateralParams array in a for-loop to validate sorting and LLTV, violating the invariant that market creation gas cost must be bounded and not exploitable to grief callers who trigger touchMarket() as a side effect of take()/repay()/withdraw(), causing scoped impact: any function that calls touchMarket() on a 128-collateral market pays O(128) gas for the initialization loop even if the market already exists? Proof idea: fuzz collateralParams.length from 1 to 128, measure gas of touchMarket() on first call vs subsequent calls, assert subsequent calls skip the loop.

[File: 'File Name: src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate / collateralBitmap while-loop] Can an unprivileged borrower under the precondition that they activate MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER (16) collaterals in a market with 128 collateral slots trigger liquidate() which iterates the full collateralBitmap in a while-loop calling IOracle.price() for each activated collateral, violating the invariant that liquidation must remain economically viable and not be blocked by gas cost, causing scoped impact: a borrower activates 16 collaterals each with a slow/expensive oracle to make liquidation gas cost prohibitive, preventing timely liquidation of unhealthy positions? Proof idea: fuzz activated collateral count from 1 to 16, measure gas of liquidate() per activated collateral, assert gas grows linearly and document the maximum gas at 16 collaterals with expensive oracles.

[File: 'File Name: src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.isHealthy / collateralBitmap while-loop] Can an unprivileged borrower under the precondition that they activate 16 collaterals trigger isHealthy() which iterates collateralBitmap in a while-loop calling IOracle.price() for each activated collateral, violating the invariant that health checks must complete within a predictable gas budget, causing scoped impact: every take() call where the seller has 16 activated collaterals pays O(16) oracle calls at the end of take() via the SellerIsLiquidatable check, enabling a seller to grief takers by maximizing activated collaterals? Proof idea: fuzz seller's activated collateral count from 0 to 16, measure gas of take() at the isHealthy() check, assert gas grows linearly with activated collateral count.

[File: 'File Name: src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.multicall] Can an unprivileged caller under the precondition that they construct a calls[] array of arbitrary length trigger multicall() which iterates calls.length times with delegatecall, violating the invariant that a single transaction must not be able to consume unbounded gas through protocol-provided looping, causing scoped impact: an attacker submits a multicall with thousands of no-op or cheap calls to consume the entire block gas limit, blocking other users' transactions in the same block? Proof idea: fuzz calls.length from 1 to 10000, measure gas consumed by multicall() with minimal inner calls, assert there is no upper bound check on calls.length and document the maximum calls before block gas limit.

[File: 'File Name: src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.flashLoan] Can an unprivileged caller under the precondition that they supply tokens[] and assets[] arrays of arbitrary length trigger flashLoan() which iterates tokens.length twice (once to send, once to receive), violating the invariant that flash loan gas cost must be bounded and not exploitable for block stuffing, causing scoped impact: an attacker submits a flashLoan with a very large tokens[] array to consume block gas, or to force the callback to process an unbounded number of token transfers? Proof idea: fuzz tokens.length from 1 to 1000, measure gas of flashLoan() per token, assert gas grows linearly and document the maximum tokens before block gas limit.

[File: 'File Name: src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: SetterRatifier.isRatified + HashLib.hashMarket + Midnight.isHealthy] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that a market has 128 collaterals, the seller has 16 activated collaterals, and a SetterRatifier with depth-20 proof is used trigger take() which sequentially executes hashOffer() (128-collateral loop) + isHealthy() (16-collateral oracle loop), violating the invariant that the combined gas cost of a single take() must not be exploitable to exceed block gas limits, causing scoped impact: composed gas griefing where market creator and borrower cooperate to make take() calls in their market prohibitively expensive? Proof idea: create 128-collateral market, seller activates 16 collaterals, use SetterRatifier with depth-20 proof, measure total take() gas and assert it fits within block gas limit.

[File: 'File Name: src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can an unprivileged caller under the precondition that they compose a multicall with N take() calls each using a 128-collateral market and SetterRatifier with depth-20 proofs trigger multicall() → N × (hashOffer(128 collaterals) + isHealthy(16 collaterals)), violating the invariant that multicall must not amplify per-call gas costs to block-filling levels, causing scoped impact: a single multicall transaction can be crafted to consume the entire block gas limit through legitimate protocol operations, constituting a low-cost DoS? Proof idea: fuzz N from 1 to 100, measure total gas of multicall with max-collateral take() calls, assert the gas-per-call overhead and find the N at which block gas limit is reached.

[File: 'File Name: src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.touchMarket + HashLib.hashMarket] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that a market with 128 collaterals has not yet been touched trigger take() → touchMarket() which runs the 128-collateral initialization loop AND isRatified() → hashOffer() → hashMarket() which runs another 128-collateral loop in the same transaction, violating the invariant that the first take() on a new market must not be disproportionately expensive compared to subsequent takes(), causing scoped impact: the first taker in a 128-collateral market pays double the collateral-loop gas cost, which could be used to grief specific takers? Proof idea: compare gas of take() on untouched vs already-touched 128-collateral market with SetterRatifier, assert the gas difference equals the touchMarket initialization loop cost.

[File: 'File Name: src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.withdrawCollateral + isHealthy] Can an unprivileged borrower under the precondition that they have 16 activated collaterals trigger withdrawCollateral() → isHealthy() which iterates all 16 activated collaterals calling IOracle.price() each time, violating the invariant that collateral withdrawal gas cost must be bounded and not exploitable by the borrower to grief authorized operators, causing scoped impact: a borrower with 16 activated collaterals makes every withdrawCollateral() call by any authorized operator prohibitively expensive? Proof idea: fuzz activated collateral count from 1 to 16, measure gas of withdrawCollateral() at the isHealthy() check, assert gas grows linearly.

[File: 'File Name: src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: HashLib.hashMarket / collateralParamsHashes dynamic array] Can an unprivileged market creator under the precondition that a market with 128 collaterals is created trigger hashMarket() which allocates a dynamic bytes32[] array of length 128 and iterates over it, violating the invariant that memory allocation in hashMarket() must not cause quadratic memory expansion costs when called repeatedly in a multicall, causing scoped impact: repeated calls to isRatified() in a multicall each allocate O(128) memory, and Ethereum's quadratic memory cost model makes later calls in the multicall disproportionately expensive? Proof idea: measure gas of the Nth take() call in a multicall with 128-collateral market, assert gas per call increases due to memory expansion, and compare to a 1-collateral market.

[File: 'File Name: src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: SetterRatifier.setIsRootRatified + Midnight.isAuthorized] Can an unprivileged authorized operator under the precondition that a maker has authorized them via setIsAuthorized() trigger setIsRootRatified() on behalf of the maker for an arbitrary number of roots in a loop, violating the invariant that root ratification state changes must not be exploitable to grief the maker by filling storage with ratified roots, causing scoped impact: an authorized operator ratifies thousands of roots for the maker, making the maker's storage state expensive to reason about and potentially enabling future griefing via root revocation? Proof idea: fuzz the number of setIsRootRatified() calls by an authorized operator, assert each call costs O(1) storage but document the total gas cost of revoking all roots.

[File: 'File Name: src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate / badDebt while-loop + continuousFeeCredit update] Can an unprivileged liquidator under the precondition that a borrower has 16 activated collaterals and significant bad debt trigger liquidate() which iterates the full collateralBitmap to compute maxDebt and badDebt, then updates lossFactor and continuousFeeCredit, violating the invariant that bad debt realization gas cost must not be exploitable to prevent liquidation of positions with many collaterals, causing scoped impact: a borrower with 16 collaterals and bad debt cannot be liquidated if the gas cost of the while-loop plus oracle calls exceeds the liquidator's gas budget? Proof idea: create a borrower with 16 activated collaterals all with bad debt, measure gas of liquidate() with seizedAssets=0 and repaidUnits=0 (bad debt only), assert gas fits within block gas limit.

[File: 'File Name: src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.supplyCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower under the precondition that a market has 128 collateral slots trigger a multicall with 16 supplyCollateral() calls to activate 16 different collaterals in a single transaction, violating the invariant that the collateralBitmap activation limit (MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER=16) must be enforced atomically and not bypassable through multicall ordering, causing scoped impact: if the bitmap check in supplyCollateral() is not atomic across multicall steps, a borrower could activate more than 16 collaterals? Proof idea: compose a multicall with 17 supplyCollateral() calls for different collateral indices, assert the 17th call reverts with TooManyActivatedCollaterals().

[File: 'File Name: src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.take + isHealthy seller check + multicall] Can an unprivileged seller under the precondition that they have 16 activated collaterals trigger a multicall with N take() calls where they are the seller in each, violating the invariant that the isHealthy() check at the end of each take() must not be amplified by multicall to create O(N×16) oracle calls in a single transaction, causing scoped impact: a seller with 16 collaterals who is the maker in many offers forces every taker to pay 16 oracle calls per take(), and a multicall of N takes costs O(N×16) oracle calls? Proof idea: fuzz N from 1 to 50, measure total oracle calls in a multicall of N take() calls with a 16-collateral seller, assert oracle call count equals N×activated_collaterals.

[File: 'File Name: src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: HashLib.isLeaf + leafIndex validation] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that they supply a ratifierData with proof.length=20 and leafIndex with bit 20 set trigger isLeaf() which checks leafIndex >> proof.length == 0, violating the invariant that the LeafIndexOutOfRange check must correctly reject all invalid leaf indices regardless of proof length, causing scoped impact: if the bit-shift check is off-by-one, a taker could supply an out-of-range leafIndex that passes validation, potentially allowing proof forgery for adjacent leaves? Proof idea: fuzz leafIndex and proof.length combinations near the boundary (leafIndex == 2^proof.length - 1, leafIndex == 2^proof.length), assert LeafIndexOutOfRange() is thrown exactly when leafIndex >= 2^proof.length.

[File: 'File Name: src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: SetterRatifier.isRatified + Midnight.take multicall composition] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that they compose a multicall with M take() calls each using a different 128-collateral market with SetterRatifier and depth-20 proofs trigger M × (hashOffer(128) + isLeaf(20) + isHealthy(16)) gas consumption, violating the invariant that multicall must not allow a single caller to consume the entire block gas budget through legitimate protocol calls, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS where an attacker with minimal capital can fill the block with a single multicall transaction? Proof idea: calculate theoretical maximum gas for M takes with max parameters, find M at which block gas limit is reached, assert this M is achievable with minimal economic cost.

[File: 'File Name: src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.touchMarket / collateralParams sorting loop] Can an unprivileged market creator under the precondition that they call touchMarket() with a Market struct containing 128 collateralParams where all tokens are valid but sorted in descending order trigger the sorting validation loop which iterates all 128 entries before reverting with CollateralParamsNotSorted(), violating the invariant that invalid market creation attempts must not consume significant gas, causing scoped impact: an attacker repeatedly calls touchMarket() with 128-collateral markets that fail validation at the last element, consuming O(128) gas per failed attempt to grief the mempool? Proof idea: measure gas of touchMarket() that fails CollateralParamsNotSorted() at index 127 vs index 0, assert gas consumed is proportional to the index at which sorting fails.

[File: 'File Name: src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate + isHealthy cross-call] Can an unprivileged liquidator under the precondition that a borrower has 16 activated collaterals and is unhealthy trigger liquidate() (16-collateral while-loop) followed immediately by isHealthy() (another 16-collateral while-loop) in a multicall to verify post-liquidation health, violating the invariant that the combined gas cost of liquidate() + health verification must not exceed block gas limits for max-collateral positions, causing scoped impact: liquidators of 16-collateral positions cannot profitably liquidate because gas cost exceeds liquidation incentive? Proof idea: measure gas of liquidate() + isHealthy() for borrowers with 1, 4, 8, 12, 16 activated collaterals, assert profitability threshold and document the collateral count at which liquidation becomes unprofitable.

[File: 'File Name: src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator under the precondition that there are N unhealthy borrowers each with 16 activated collaterals trigger a multicall with N liquidate() calls, violating the invariant that batch liquidation via multicall must not be blocked by cumulative gas cost of N×16 oracle calls, causing scoped impact: a liquidator attempting to batch-liquidate many positions in a single multicall is blocked by gas limits, while individual liquidations remain viable, creating a liquidation efficiency gap? Proof idea: fuzz N from 1 to 50, measure gas of multicall with N liquidate() calls on 16-collateral borrowers, assert the maximum N before block gas limit and compare to single-call liquidation gas.

[File: 'File Name: src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: HashLib.hashOffer + callbackData keccak256] Can an unprivileged maker under the precondition that they include a very large callbackData bytes field in their Offer struct trigger hashOffer() → keccak256(offer.callbackData) which hashes the full callbackData, violating the invariant that offer hashing gas cost must be bounded and not controllable by the maker to grief takers, causing scoped impact: a maker publishes an offer with a multi-megabyte callbackData field, making every isRatified() call for that offer prohibitively expensive for takers? Proof idea: fuzz offer.callbackData.length from 0 to 100KB, measure gas of hashOffer() and isRatified(), assert gas grows linearly with callbackData size and document the maximum size before block gas limit.

[File: 'File Name: src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.take + ratifierData abi.decode] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that they supply a very large ratifierData bytes field to take() trigger abi.decode(ratifierData, (bytes32, uint256, bytes32[])) in isRatified() which decodes the full bytes, violating the invariant that taker-supplied data must not cause unbounded gas consumption in the ratifier, causing scoped impact: a taker supplies a malformed but large ratifierData that passes abi.decode but causes excessive gas consumption before the InvalidProof() revert, enabling self-griefing or gas estimation manipulation? Proof idea: fuzz ratifierData.length with valid prefix but trailing garbage, measure gas consumed before revert, assert gas is bounded by proof.length not ratifierData.length.

[File: 'File Name: src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + SetterRatifier.setIsRootRatified] Can an unprivileged authorized operator under the precondition that they are authorized for a maker trigger a multicall with N setIsRootRatified() calls to ratify N different roots in a single transaction, violating the invariant that bulk root ratification must not be exploitable to front-run or grief the maker's offer management, causing scoped impact: an authorized operator ratifies a large number of roots atomically, then immediately revokes them all in a second multicall, creating a window where offers appear valid but are immediately invalidated? Proof idea: compose a multicall of N setIsRootRatified(true) calls followed by N setIsRootRatified(false) calls, assert the net state is all roots unratified and measure gas cost of the attack.
